title: Form follows function for RNA editing - Nature Biotechnology
text:
Access through your institution

Buy or subscribe

A-to-I RNA editing with endogenous ADAR is a promising therapeutic tool considered a safer alternative to permanent DNA alterations. While advantageous, this strategy remains limited by widespread bystander editing and target restrictions due to ADAR’s intrinsic sequence preferences. Song et al. systematically studied the underlying mechanism of ADAR binding to double-stranded RNA (dsRNA) and applied these principles to develop an RNA-editing tool with improved precision and an extended target range.ADAR-recruiting RNAs (arRNAs) are crucial for endogenous ADAR editing. Using AlphaFold 3, the authors found that only a small segment of the extended RNA structure is catalytically relevant within a defined interaction window. After examining the effect of RNA structure on editing efficiency and testing various positions of RNA bulges within the arRNA design, they were able to enhance arRNA precision by placing bulges within the binding regions and then refining efficiency by positioning bulges in the peripheral ADAR-binding areas.
